message:
stdio ABI: sync copy-relocated stdin/stdout/stderr symbols from init_array

Rewire the frankenlibc-abi .init_array entry so process startup patches the
host glibc copy-relocated stdio symbol cells in addition to running the
exit-safe libio bootstrap. This fixes the long-standing case where consumer
binaries that take the address of `stdin`/`stdout`/`stderr` (or the _IO_2_1_*
aliases) get copy-relocated cells that never observe our NativeFile-backed
FILE objects, even though our ABI exports them with the correct addresses.

- crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/lib.rs: promote FRANKENLIBC_ABI_INIT_ARRAY to
  pub + #[no_mangle] so the init_array entry has a stable external symbol,
  and retarget it to the new stdio_abi::init_host_stdio_streams() entry
  point. The previous implementation only ran bootstrap_host_libio_exports;
  the new one covers both host-exit safety and stdio stream publication.

- crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/stdio_abi.rs: add
  sync_copy_relocated_stdio_symbol() which resolves the host glibc copy of
  each stdio symbol (via dlsym with RTLD_DEFAULT fallback) and writes our
  owned NativeFile pointer into that cell, so dynamic linker-assigned copies
  converge on the same FILE *. Gated on #[cfg(not(debug_assertions))] so it
  only activates in release builds where copy-relocation actually occurs.
  Covers both the short names (stdin/stdout/stderr) and the _IO_2_1_* aliases
  consumed by older glibc-generated call sites.

- scripts/check_stdio_native.sh: make the release-library locator accept
  either the cargo-metadata target_directory or the repo-relative
  target/release path (so the script works under both rch and local
  release builds), and pass -Wno-format-truncation when compiling the
  integration fixtures to silence noisy but harmless glibc 2.39 warnings
  on snprintf into bounded buffers. Also fixes the symbol address awk
  so it finds the first definition instead of exiting on the first match
  of the name pattern including suffixes.

Leaves ad-hoc test_fts_*/test_scanf/test_wordexp prototype files in the
working tree uncommitted (ephemeral local experiments for fts/scanf/wordexp
debugging, not part of the test suite).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -13,12 +13,23 @@ RUN_ID="stdio-native-$(date -u +%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ)-$$"
 mkdir -p "${OUT_DIR}" "${BIN_DIR}"
 
 TARGET_DIR="$(cargo metadata --format-version 1 --no-deps | jq -r '.target_directory')"
-LIB="${TARGET_DIR}/release/libfrankenlibc_abi.so"
+LIB_CANDIDATES=(
+  "${TARGET_DIR}/release/libfrankenlibc_abi.so"
+  "${ROOT}/target/release/libfrankenlibc_abi.so"
+)
+LIB=""
 
 echo "[stdio-native] building release ABI library via rch" >"${TEST_LOG}"
 rch exec -- cargo build -p frankenlibc-abi --release >>"${TEST_LOG}" 2>&1
 
-if [[ ! -f "${LIB}" ]]; then
+for candidate in "${LIB_CANDIDATES[@]}"; do
+  if [[ -f "${candidate}" ]]; then
+    LIB="${candidate}"
+    break
+  fi
+done
+
+if [[ -z "${LIB}" ]]; then
   echo "FAIL: expected release library missing at ${LIB}" >&2
   cat "${TEST_LOG}" >&2
   exit 1
@@ -30,9 +41,13 @@ symbol_addr() {
     {
       name = $NF
       sub(/@.*/, "", name)
-      if (name == sym) {
-        print $1
-        exit
+      if (name == sym && addr == "") {
+        addr = $1
+      }
+    }
+    END {
+      if (addr != "") {
+        print addr
       }
     }
   '
@@ -60,9 +75,9 @@ check_alias_addr "stdin" "_IO_2_1_stdin_"
 check_alias_addr "stdout" "_IO_2_1_stdout_"
 check_alias_addr "stderr" "_IO_2_1_stderr_"
 
-cc -D_GNU_SOURCE -D_POSIX_C_SOURCE=200809L -std=c11 -O2 "${ROOT}/tests/integration/fixture_stdio_printf.c" \
+cc -D_GNU_SOURCE -D_POSIX_C_SOURCE=200809L -std=c11 -O2 -Wno-format-truncation "${ROOT}/tests/integration/fixture_stdio_printf.c" \
   -o "${BIN_DIR}/fixture_stdio_printf.bin"
-cc -D_POSIX_C_SOURCE=200809L -std=c11 -O2 "${ROOT}/tests/integration/fixture_stdio_globals.c" \
+cc -D_POSIX_C_SOURCE=200809L -std=c11 -O2 -Wno-format-truncation "${ROOT}/tests/integration/fixture_stdio_globals.c" \
   -o "${BIN_DIR}/fixture_stdio_globals.bin"
 
 now_iso_ms() {
